subtopics: Hardware, software, input/output devices, CPU, memory units (bit to PB)
System software: OS, utilities, drivers; Programming tools and translators
OS user interface and core functions
key_concepts: Boolean logic operations (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR) and De Morgan's laws
Number system conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, Hexadecimal
Encoding schemes: ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32)

subtopics: Python environment: interactive/script modes, tokens, variables (l-value/r-value)
Data types: Number, Boolean, Sequence (String, List, Tuple), Mapping (Dictionary)
key_concepts: Operators and Expressions: Arithmetic, Relational, Logical, Identity, Membership; Precedence
Flow of Control: Conditional statements (if-else) and Iterative loops (for/while)
Data Structures Operations: String manipulation methods, List/Tuple/Dict built-in functions

subtopics: Digital footprints, net etiquettes, social media communication norms
Data Protection: IPR (Copyright, Patent), Open Source Licensing (GPL, Apache)
Cyber Crime types: Hacking, Phishing, Ransomware, Cyber bullying
key_concepts: Cyber Safety and Malware protection strategies
Information Technology Act (IT Act) compliance
Technology impact on society regarding gender and disability issues

subtopics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI
key_concepts: Functions: types (built-in, module-defined, user-defined), arguments and parameters (default, positional), return values, flow of execution, scope (global/local)
Exception Handling: try-except-finally blocks

File Handling: Text files (modes r/r+/w/w+/a/a+, with clause, read/write methods, seek/tell), Binary files (rb/rb+/wb/wb+/ab/ab+, pickle module dump/load/search/update), CSV files (csv module writer/read operations)
Data Structure: Stack implementation using list (push/pop)

subtopics: Evolution of networking: ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET
key_concepts: Data communication terminologies: components (sender, receiver, message, media, protocols), bandwidth, data transfer rate, IP address, switching techniques
Transmission media: Wired (Twisted pair, Co-axial, Fiber-optic) and Wireless (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared)
Network devices: Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card
Network topologies and types: PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN; Bus, Star, Tree
Protocols and Web Services: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP, WWW, HTML, XML, URL

subtopics: Relational data model: relation, attribute, tuple, domain, degree, cardinality, keys (candidate, primary, alternate, foreign)
key_concepts: SQL Data Definition Language and Manipulation Language
Data types and constraints: char(n), varchar(n), int, float, date; not null, unique, primary key
Database operations: create/use/drop database/tables, alter table (add/remove attributes/keys)
Querying data: insert, delete, select with operators (mathematical, relational, logical), aliasing, distinct, where clause, in/between/order by, is null/is not null, like

Advanced SQL and Python Integration: aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count), group by/having clauses, joins (cartesian, equi-join, natural join); connecting SQL with Python using cursor(), execute(), commit()
